description: Canonical KnitBit character-builder manifest. Single source of truth for the base rig, every trait's source mesh + fit transform, and the named preset loadouts. Read by tools/knitbit_manifest.py (pure Python), tools/build_character.py (Blender assembler), and tools/fit_traits.py. See docs/KnitBit-Base-Spec.md sections 4-6.

base.comment: char_height / half_width come from the base mesh bounds printed by tools/rig_knitbit.py (v2 base: min=(-0.659,-0.373,-0.957) max=(0.657,0.374,0.955), Blender Z-up). All fit.scale values are fractions of char_height; fit.offset_frac is in socket-local space, also as fractions of char_height.

mesh_regions._comment: Absolute base-mesh vertex windows (same coordinate space as base.char_height/half_width) used to HIDE base geometry for a mount:limb_pair trait with a `hides` region — this is what makes boots/hands a true mesh-SWAP instead of a socketed overlay (no double-boot/double-hand). Measured once by probing knitbit_base_rigged.glb's vertex positions per limb (see docs/KnitBit-Base-Spec.md section 10). Windows are given for the LEFT (+x) side; the RIGHT side mirrors by negating x_min/x_max. build_character.py deletes any base vertex whose (x, z) falls inside the window via bmesh before mounting the replacement mesh — additive at build time, the committed base GLB is never modified.

foot.comment: Isolates the base's entire sculpted boot for the mesh-swap. v2 probe: the leg's narrow waist is z -0.48..-0.52 (x_max ~0.25); the base boot-top flare starts at ~-0.54 and the knee plate ends by -0.46. Cutting at -0.50 removes the whole sculpted boot; the replacement boot at scale 0.26 (cuff top ~-0.46) covers the cut edge. Cut must stay within the narrow waist band or base boot flare pokes past the prop cuff (seen at -0.56 and -0.60).

hand.comment: No z bound needed: in the A-pose this x-band contains ONLY the hand/fist (v2 base probed z range -0.563..-0.136). x_min raised 0.5->0.55 for the v2 base because its head ear plates reach |x|=0.521 at z_frac 0.70-0.80 and would otherwise fall inside the band.

antenna.label: Antenna
antenna.comment: mount:antenna_pair traits fit on socket_head_left_antenna + socket_head_right_antenna (right mirrored on x).

helmet_panel.label: Head Top Panel
helmet_panel.comment: Low-profile surface detail on socket_head_top_center only. Never dome/helmet geometry (the head IS the helmet, spec section 1).

belt_charm.label: Belt Charm
belt_charm.comment: All belt charms share a fit.offset_frac that hangs them off the belt on the character's RIGHT hip (toward socket_belt_right at x=-0.234, ~87% of the way; character's right = -x), with the hook clipped onto the belt band and the body dangling FREELY below, clear of the thigh. The offset: -x toward the right hip (away from center-front), -y just proud enough to clear the thigh's forward bulge (leg front ~-0.154 below the belt), +z so the hanger ring clips onto the belt band's edge rather than floating below it or riding above it. Identical across charms so the placement reads consistently.

dynamic.comment: Belt charms are a DYNAMIC (free-hanging) attachment: the runtime should swing them as a pendulum about the hook when the character moves. build_character.py sets the charm node's origin to the hook (top-center) and writes these params to the node's glTF extras (knitbit_* keys) so the engine's spring/verlet system (see game/lib/physics/verlet_rope.dart) can drive it. Static preview renders show the rest pose; the swing itself is runtime.

accessory.label: Accessory
accessory.comment: Each accessory carries its own socket (backpack -> back_center, headphones -> head_top_center, watering can -> right_hand_grip).

boots.label: Boots
boots.comment: TRUE MESH-SWAP (spec section 5's 'Skinned / mesh-swapped lower-leg + foot part'), not a socketed overlay: mount:limb_pair with hides:'foot' deletes the base's own boot geometry (mesh_regions.foot) before attaching the replacement, mirrored L/R, at the Foot.L/Foot.R bone heads. Without the hide, the base's baked-in boot would double up with the new one (same class of bug as the original helmet-on-helmet issue).

hands.label: Hands
hands.comment: TRUE MESH-SWAP, same mechanism as boots: mount:limb_pair with hides:'hand' deletes the base's own hand geometry (mesh_regions.hand) before attaching the replacement at the Hand.L/Hand.R bone heads.

themes._comment: The spec-section-7 theme legend as data. Neutral base = charcoal yarn; each theme is produced later by a yarn material-swap to `accent` plus theme-specific accessories. `accent` doubles as the label/generation reference color and drives the Master Trait Diagram swatches. Each trait carries a `theme` tag keyed to these.

traits.label: Scout Camera
traits.note: 20-deg outward (Y) lean + 0.13 scale keep the camera modules off the base's baked-in antenna nubs and out of the variant_b headphone band. The lean is symmetric under the mirrored -x scale; an X tilt is not.

traits.label: Leaf Tip
traits.note: 0.13 scale so the leaf tips clear the base's baked-in antenna nubs.

traits.label: Reinforced
traits.note: Small -0.01 z offset sinks the plate edges into the curved crown instead of hovering.

traits.label: Leaf
traits.note: This badge reconstructed lying flat (emblem up) unlike the standing wrench badge, so it needs a real +90 X pitch to face the emblem front (verified against -90/180).

traits.label: Gear
traits.note: offset_frac hangs the charm on the front-right of the belt toward the right hip (see the belt_charm slot comment). v2 retune: hook raised +0.04 world z and pushed +0.025 toward the body so the ring top visibly overlaps the belt band instead of floating in front of it.

traits.label: Coin
traits.note: offset_frac hangs the charm on the front-right of the belt toward the right hip (see the belt_charm slot comment). v2 retune: hook raised +0.04 world z and pushed +0.025 toward the body so the ring top visibly overlaps the belt band instead of floating in front of it.

traits.label: Flower
traits.note: offset_frac hangs the charm on the front-right of the belt toward the right hip (see the belt_charm slot comment). v2 retune: hook raised +0.04 world z and pushed +0.025 toward the body so the ring top visibly overlaps the belt band instead of floating in front of it.

traits.label: Headphones
traits.note: v2 retune: scale 0.62->0.50 (user: too big) with z offset -0.10 so the band hugs the crown while clearing both antenna variants.

traits.label: Watering Can
traits.note: First handheld: parents to a hand-grip socket rather than a surface socket.

traits.label: Classic
traits.note: v2 retune r5: boots centered on the SILHOUETTE center (bbox mid) of the leg segment actually visible between the brief hem and the cuff top (z -0.42..-0.37): L +0.146, R -0.146. x_mean is biased inboard by the inner-thigh yarn drape; the bbox center is what reads as the leg's centerline from the front.

traits.label: Classic
traits.note: v2 base retune: rotation re-derived from the new Hand.L bone dir (knuckle-local -Y -> bone head->tail), scale 0.2->0.16 for the slimmer v2 arm, offset re-measured so the mitt centroid covers the hidden hand blob centroid (0.596,-0.011,-0.454) instead of the forearm.
